documents.title: Beyond Gensets: Advancing the Energy Transition in Lagos State
documents.classification: Confidential/Proprietary — SEforALL. Used for internal AI grounding only. No reproduction.
documents.focus: Lagos State fossil fuel genset study — quantity, usage patterns, environmental and economic impacts

policy_promises.promise: State-wide distributed solar PV programme to replace fossil fuel gensets
policy_promises.source_policy: Lagos State Government energy policy intent

policy_promises.promise: Fossil fuel genset displacement programme informed by comprehensive data
policy_promises.source_policy: SEforALL / Lagos State Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources

grid_access_and_reliability.finding: 98% of households are connected to the grid
grid_access_and_reliability.context: Connection rate is high but actual supply is critically insufficient

grid_access_and_reliability.finding: Only ~20% of Lagos electricity demand is met by the grid
grid_access_and_reliability.context: The remaining 80% deficit is largely covered by gensets

grid_access_and_reliability.finding: 37% of households receive 9–12 hours of grid supply daily
grid_access_and_reliability.context: Most common supply band; 13% receive less than 4 hours daily

grid_access_and_reliability.finding: 6% of households receive more than 18 hours of grid supply daily
grid_access_and_reliability.context: Reliable supply is a minority experience

grid_access_and_reliability.finding: 25% of households do not know their tariff band
grid_access_and_reliability.context: Indicates low awareness of energy policy among consumers

grid_access_and_reliability.finding: 41% of MSMEs are not connected to the grid at all
grid_access_and_reliability.context: Of those without connection, 91% cite unreliability as the reason

genset_ownership_and_usage.finding: 72% of Lagos households own at least one genset
84% of households purchased gensets primarily due to grid unreliability
genset_ownership_and_usage.context: Grid failure is the dominant driver of genset adoption

genset_ownership_and_usage.finding: 68% of households cope with power outages using backup gensets
genset_ownership_and_usage.context: Second most common coping strategy is portable lighting (25%)

genset_ownership_and_usage.finding: 94% of MSMEs own at least one genset
13% of households use gensets for income generation
genset_ownership_and_usage.context: Gensets serve dual residential and economic functions

genset_ownership_and_usage.finding: Most residential gensets are under 5kVA and fuelled by petrol (97.7%)
Commercial gensets predominantly use diesel (59%), followed by petrol (38%)
genset_ownership_and_usage.context: Commercial gensets often operate as primary — not backup — power sources

genset_ownership_and_usage.finding: 73% of residential genset peak use occurs at night (8pm–12am)
genset_ownership_and_usage.context: Reflects gensets filling the gap when grid supply is lowest

genset_ownership_and_usage.finding: 67% of MSMEs experience peak load demand in the afternoon (12pm–4pm)
genset_ownership_and_usage.context: Commercial and residential genset use patterns differ significantly

economic_burden.finding: Lagos households collectively spent ~₦532 billion acquiring current gensets
economic_burden.context: Range ₦338bn–₦725bn depending on purchase cost assumptions

economic_burden.finding: Households annually spend ~₦1.43 trillion on genset fuel
economic_burden.context: Based on 9 billion litres consumed at average Lagos petrol prices

economic_burden.finding: Households annually spend ~₦192 billion on genset maintenance
economic_burden.context: Combined fuel and maintenance cost vastly exceeds grid electricity cost

economic_burden.finding: Average cost of genset electricity exceeds grid supply cost by over ₦2,000 per hour
economic_burden.context: Pre-subsidy removal estimate; current costs are likely higher

economic_burden.finding: Petrol genset electricity costs ~83% more than grid electricity
economic_burden.context: Cost estimates pre-date subsidy removal; actual gap is now wider

economic_burden.finding: Average small business spends ₦20,000–40,000 per month on genset fuel alone
economic_burden.context: Fuel cost is the single largest operating cost for many SMEs

economic_burden.finding: Nigerians spend ~$10 billion (₦7.6 trillion) annually on small petrol gensets nationwide
MSMEs spend ~₦5.3 trillion on genset fuel annually
economic_burden.context: Diesel and petrol users combined across 80,072 MSMEs in Lagos State

subsidy_removal_impact.finding: 84% of households reduced typical hours of genset use after petrol subsidy removal
subsidy_removal_impact.context: Only 2% switched to renewables; 9% went without electricity during outages

subsidy_removal_impact.finding: 79% of commercial enterprises reduced typical hours of genset usage post-subsidy removal
subsidy_removal_impact.context: Subsidy removal significantly disrupted business energy access

subsidy_removal_impact.finding: 28% of households have specifically considered alternative energy since subsidy removal
subsidy_removal_impact.context: Subsidy removal created some openness to solar transition

health_and_environment.finding: ~1,500 deaths per year nationally from inhaling genset smoke and carbon monoxide
health_and_environment.context: Direct health toll of grid failure and genset dependence

health_and_environment.finding: 70% increased risk of lung cancer from genset smoke exposure
health_and_environment.context: Long-term health burden concentrated in urban households

health_and_environment.finding: More than 2 in 3 genset users report impaired hearing
health_and_environment.context: Noise pollution is a pervasive but underreported consequence

health_and_environment.finding: Lagos genset emissions: ~39 million tCO2e annually
health_and_environment.context: Nearly 4x the total annual emissions of Togo, Rwanda, or Gabon individually

health_and_environment.finding: Residential gensets produce ~21 million tCO2e per year
health_and_environment.context: From 9 billion litres of fuel consumed annually

health_and_environment.finding: Commercial gensets produce ~17.8 million tCO2e per year
health_and_environment.context: Despite fewer units than residential, emissions are nearly equal due to higher utilization

health_and_environment.finding: 55% of households believe gensets contribute to air pollution
health_and_environment.context: 60% believe gensets significantly contribute to noise pollution

solar_transition_barriers.finding: 88% of households would consider alternative energy if available
solar_transition_barriers.context: Demand for cleaner alternatives is very high

solar_transition_barriers.finding: 75% of households are interested in solar; 70% are aware of solar power
solar_transition_barriers.context: Awareness and interest are not translating into adoption

solar_transition_barriers.finding: Only 2% of households currently use solar power
solar_transition_barriers.context: The gap between interest and adoption is enormous

solar_transition_barriers.finding: 84% of non-adopters cite high upfront capital cost as the reason for not adopting solar
solar_transition_barriers.context: The primary barrier is financial, not awareness or interest

solar_transition_barriers.finding: At point of purchase, gensets appear 10–15x cheaper than equivalent solar PV systems
solar_transition_barriers.context: Over a 10-year total cost of ownership, solar is only ~20% more expensive

solar_transition_barriers.finding: 58% of households prefer a lease-to-own model for solar ownership
Only 3% of MSMEs currently use solar power despite 64% expressing interest

policy_vs_reality_gaps.reality: Only 20% of demand met by grid; 37% of households receive less than 12 hours daily
Genset electricity costs ~83% more than grid; households spend ₦1.43 trillion/year on fuel alone

policy_vs_reality_gaps.promise: Fossil fuel displacement programme; solar PV rollout
policy_vs_reality_gaps.reality: Only 2% solar adoption; upfront cost barrier unaddressed; 4.3 million gensets still operating
1,500 deaths/year; hearing impairment in 2/3 of users; 70% increased lung cancer risk
Lagos gensets alone emit ~39 million tCO2e/year — 4x the emissions of entire African nations
Prior to this 2024 study, all existing datasets were fragmented, outdated, or methodologically limited

study_methodology_notes.ml_extrapolation: Fraym AI/ML software used to extrapolate residential data to full Lagos State at 1km2 resolution
